body:
## Summary

Extracts the repeated `withIssueLock → withStageHooks → withWorktree → scrubOutputDir → runPrompt → retryOnInvalidOutput → processResult` skeleton from the five stage commands into a single layered helper in `@baremetallabs-ai/shipper-core`. `runStageScaffold` owns the invariant lifecycle; `simpleInvoker` and `transportInvoker` express the per-stage differences by composition. Pure refactor — no user-visible behavior change.

Closes #630

## Changes

- Added `packages/core/src/lib/stage-scaffold.ts` with `runStageScaffold`, `simpleInvoker`, `transportInvoker`, and the supporting `StageScaffoldOpts` / `StageInvokerFactory` / `StageInvocation` types; re-exported from `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
- Refactored `design`, `plan`, `implement`, `pr-open`, and `pr-review` to delegate the shared scaffold to `runStageScaffold` while keeping each command's auto-selection, logging, and pre-lock/in-lock resolution order unchanged.
- Preserved load-bearing per-stage differences: `implement` uses `pushMode: 'new-branch'` with `createBranch: true`; `pr-open` keeps `resolveRef` / `tryResolvePrForIssue` / `resolveBaseBranch` before the lock, uses `pushMode: 'force-with-lease'` with `createBranch: false`, and still includes `baseBranch` in the `runPrompt('pr_open', ...)` payload; `pr-review` still writes `pr-diff.patch`, `pr-files.json`, `pr-metadata.json` via a `setup()` hook and forwards `prFiles` and `diffHunks` into retry validation.
- Used `prNumber: { value: string | undefined }` object-wrapper so `pr-open`'s `processResult` call keeps the `prNumber: undefined` own-key shape the current test asserts; design/plan/implement continue to omit the key.
- Rewrote the five per-command CLI tests to assert `runStageScaffold` args + `resolveLocked` sequencing + invoker payload parity, and added `packages/core/tests/lib/stage-scaffold.test.ts` to lock in the scaffold's lifecycle order and crash/propagate branches.
- `pr-remediate.ts` and `unblock.ts` are unchanged; the seven underlying primitives (`withIssueLock`, `withStageHooks`, `withWorktree`, `withGitTransport`, `retryOnInvalidOutput`, `processResult`, `handleAgentCrash`) are unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Each of `design`, `plan`, `implement`, `pr-open`, `pr-review` produces the same `runPrompt` invocation arguments and the same surrounding side-effect sequence (lock → hooks → worktree → scrub → [transport] → prompt → retry → process-result, with crash handling on failure paths) as before.
- [x] `pr-remediate.ts` and `unblock.ts` have no diff against their pre-refactor content.
- [x] No source edits to `withIssueLock`, `withStageHooks`, `withWorktree`, `withGitTransport`, `retryOnInvalidOutput`, `processResult`, or `handleAgentCrash`.
- [x] A new or extended test per stage asserts `runPrompt` arguments and side-effect sequence and remains in the suite.
- [x] Existing integration tests under `packages/cli/tests/commands/` for the five stages pass.
- [x] `npm run lint`, `npm run type-check`, and `npm run test` all pass.
- [x] Any new public helper exported by `@baremetallabs-ai/shipper-core` is present in `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
- [x] `implement` retains `pushMode: 'new-branch'` and `createBranch: true`; `pr-open` retains `pushMode: 'force-with-lease'` and `createBranch: false`.
- [x] `pr-review` still writes `pr-diff.patch`, `pr-files.json`, and `pr-metadata.json` into the worktree before the prompt runs, and still passes `prFiles` and `diffHunks` into `retryOnInvalidOutput`.

## Test Plan

- [x] `npm run lint`
- [x] `npm run format:check`
- [x] `npm run type-check`
- [x] `npm run build`
- [x] `npm run test` (core, cli, desktop, mcp workspaces — all green)
- [x] New `packages/core/tests/lib/stage-scaffold.test.ts` covers success ordering, `initialFailure: 'crash'` non-zero, `initialFailure: 'propagate'` non-zero, and retry/processResult rejection paths.
- [x] Rewritten per-command tests assert that `design` / `plan` / `implement` resolve `baseBranch` inside `resolveLocked`, that `pr-open` resolves `resolveRef` / `tryResolvePrForIssue` / `resolveBaseBranch` before the scaffold call, and that `pr-review` preserves the diff → files → metadata setup ordering.

## Pre-merge actions

None required.

## Post-merge actions

None required.

## Notes

- `pr-open`'s `processResult` own-key parity is preserved via `prNumber: { value: existingPrNumber }` — the wrapper makes "key present with undefined value" expressible without depending on Vitest's `.toEqual` treating `{ a: undefined }` as equal to `{}`.
- `withWorktree` receives `baseBranch` for `pr-open` via the closure in `resolveLocked`, which is silently ignored because `createBranch: false` and `stage: 'pr-open'` bypass the only branches in `withWorktree` that read `baseBranch`. No observable change.
- Unblocks the downstream chain #631 (in-process API promotion), #632 (test-mock reduction), and #637 (curated public API) by establishing the stage-primitive shape they will build on.

labels.name: shipper:ready
labels.description: Ready for final review and merge

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Refactors the five prompt-driven “stage” CLI commands to share a single, layered execution scaffold in `@baremetallabs-ai/shipper-core`, aiming to preserve behavioral parity while removing duplicated lifecycle code (lock → hooks → worktree → scrub → prompt → retry → process-result, with crash handling). **Changes:** - Added `runStageScaffold` (+ `simpleInvoker` / `transportInvoker`) in core to centralize the invariant stage lifecycle while allowing per-stage composition. - Updated `design`, `plan`, `implement`, `pr-open`, and `pr-review` commands to delegate execution to the shared scaffold. - Reworked CLI command tests to assert scaffold configuration + sequencing, and added core-level tests to lock in scaffold ordering and failure-path behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | packages/core/src/lib/stage-scaffold.ts | Introduces the shared scaffold (`runStageScaffold`) and invoker helpers (`simpleInvoker`, `transportInvoker`) that express per-stage differences by composition. | | packages/core/src/index.ts | Re-exports the new scaffold module from the core barrel export. | | packages/core/tests/lib/stage-scaffold.test.ts | Adds unit tests to assert scaffold ordering, metadata forwarding, and failure/crash/propagate paths. | | packages/cli/src/commands/design.ts | Refactors `design` to use `runStageScaffold` + `simpleInvoker`. | | packages/cli/src/commands/plan.ts | Refactors `plan` to use `runStageScaffold` + `simpleInvoker`. | | packages/cli/src/commands/implement.ts | Refactors `implement` to use `runStageScaffold` + `transportInvoker` (pushMode `new-branch`). | | packages/cli/src/commands/pr-open.ts | Refactors `pr-open` to use `runStageScaffold` + `transportInvoker` (pushMode `force-with-lease`) while keeping pre-lock resolution. | | packages/cli/src/commands/pr-review.ts | Refactors `pr-review` to use `runStageScaffold` + `simpleInvoker`, preserving pre-prompt context file setup. | | packages/cli/tests/commands/design.test.ts | Updates tests to assert scaffold args, resolution sequencing, and invoker payload parity. | | packages/cli/tests/commands/plan.test.ts | Updates tests to assert scaffold args, resolution sequencing, and invoker payload parity. | | packages/cli/tests/commands/implement.test.ts | Updates tests to assert scaffold args, resolution sequencing, and invoker payload parity for transport stages. | | packages/cli/tests/commands/pr-open.test.ts | Updates tests to assert pre-lock resolution order, scaffold args, and transport invoker behavior/parity (including `prNumber` own-key shape). | | packages/cli/tests/commands/pr-review.test.ts | Updates tests to assert pre-lock ref resolution order, scaffold args, and setup/prompt invoker parity. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a centralized runStageScaffold utility in the core package to standardize the execution flow across various CLI commands, including design, plan, implement, pr-open, and pr-review. By abstracting common logic for issue locking, stage hooks, worktree management, and error handling into this shared scaffold, the individual command implementations have been significantly simplified and boilerplate code has been reduced. Corresponding tests have been updated or added to ensure the new architecture maintains existing functionality and correctly handles stage-specific configurations. I have no feedback to provide.

## Review Summary

**Verdict: APPROVE**

The refactor faithfully extracts the shared `withIssueLock → withStageHooks → withWorktree → scrubOutputDir → runPrompt → retryOnInvalidOutput → processResult` skeleton into `runStageScaffold` plus `simpleInvoker`/`transportInvoker`, and wires every command through the factory+callback pattern agreed in the fourth-pass design. I diffed each command against `main` and the pre-/in-lock resolution placement, `runPrompt` payload shape, `withGitTransport` opts, `retryOnInvalidOutput` args, and `processResult` own-key shape are all preserved. The `prNumber: { value }` wrapper correctly disambiguates "key omitted" vs "key present with undefined". `pr-remediate.ts`, `unblock.ts`, and the seven referenced primitives (`withIssueLock`, `withStageHooks`, `withWorktree`, `withGitTransport`, `retryOnInvalidOutput`, `processResult`, `handleAgentCrash`) are untouched, and the new core test pins the scaffold lifecycle ordering plus both crash/propagate branches.

### Analysis

| Dimension | Hypothesis tested | Conclusion |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Data-flow correctness | `pr-open` resolves `baseBranch` pre-lock; does the value reach both `withGitTransport` (via factory ctx) and the `runPrompt` payload (via `baseRunPromptOpts`)? | Verified: `pr-open.ts:49` returns `baseBranch` from `resolveLocked` (ctx) and `:54` embeds the same identifier in `baseRunPromptOpts`. Tests at `pr-open.test.ts:160-184` assert both. |
| Data-flow correctness | Does `setupCtx` ({prFiles, diffHunks}) flow into `retryOnInvalidOutput` for pr-review but not leak into the initial `runPrompt`? | Verified: `stage-scaffold.ts:181` spreads `setupCtx` only into the retry opts. Initial call at `:158` takes only the base payload. Matches original `pr-review.ts` behavior. |
| Edge-case resilience | If `resolveLocked` returns no `baseBranch`, is the `withWorktree` key conditionally omitted (preserving the pre-refactor pr-review/pr-open call shape for pr-review)? | Verified at `stage-scaffold.ts:149` with `...(baseBranch !== undefined ? { baseBranch } : {})`. Test `stage-scaffold.test.ts:354-363` pins absence. |
| Edge-case resilience | `pr-open` now passes `baseBranch` into `withWorktree` (previously omitted). Does `withWorktree` silently ignore it under `createBranch:false` + `stage:'pr-open'`? | Confirmed by reading `worktree.ts:64-108` (gated on `createBranch`) and `:113-125` (gated on `stage === 'design' \|\| stage === 'plan'`). Both branches are skipped for pr-open. No observable change. |
| Error-path completeness | `initialFailure: 'crash'` non-zero exit must log + `handleAgentCrash` (5-arg) + set `process.exitCode = 1`. | `stage-scaffold.ts:160-170` does exactly that; test `stage-scaffold.test.ts:245-276` asserts the 5-arg shape and exit code. Matches pre-refactor `design.ts:62-74` / `plan.ts:55-67` / `pr-review.ts:94-106`. |
| Error-path completeness | `initialFailure: 'propagate'` non-zero exit must NOT log, NOT crash, only set `process.exitCode = transportCode`. | `stage-scaffold.ts:171-173` omits logger/handleAgentCrash; test `stage-scaffold.test.ts:278-303` pins this. Matches pre-refactor transport behavior. |
| Error-path completeness | Retry/processResult throw must route through 4-arg `handleAgentCrash` (no agentSummary). | `stage-scaffold.ts:192-196` passes only 4 args via `toErrorMessage(error)`. Test `stage-scaffold.test.ts:305-336` asserts the 4-arg call. |
| Cross-caller analysis | New exports `runStageScaffold`, `simpleInvoker`, `transportInvoker`. Any callers outside the 5 stage commands that would break? | Grep confirms only the five command files import the new symbols. `pr-remediate` and `unblock` (out-of-scope) are untouched. |
| Cross-caller analysis | `transportInvoker` throws at runtime if `baseBranch === undefined` in the factory ctx. Could any caller hit this path? | Only `implement` and `pr-open` use `transportInvoker`; both always populate `baseBranch` in `resolveLocked`. Runtime precondition is a safety net, not a reachable path. |
| Key-collision and silent-overwrite safety | N/A — no map keys, file-name generation, or shared-resource writes introduced. | N/A |
| Accessibility | N/A — no UI changes. | N/A |
| Environmental assumptions | The scaffold uses `process.exitCode = N`. Does it assume an environment where the process isn't already exited/failed? | `process.exitCode` is a Node.js-standard setter; callers exit only when Node's event loop drains. Matches existing pattern. Scaffold tests guard state in `beforeEach`/`afterEach`. |
| Environmental assumptions | CLI tests mix `vi.mock('@baremetallabs-ai/shipper-core')` with `vi.importActual('../../../core/src/lib/stage-scaffold.js')`. Does the mocked `prompt-runner` seam at the relative path apply when the real invoker indirectly calls `runPrompt`? | Vitest applies `vi.mock('<path>')` globally by resolved module identity. The real `simpleInvoker`/`transportInvoker` re-enter the mocked `prompt-runner` — validated by the prompt-payload assertions passing in each test. |
| Prompt-text accuracy | N/A — no `.md` prompt files changed. | N/A |

### Findings (0 total)

- 🔴 0 must-fix
- 🟡 0 should-fix
- 🟢 1 nit

See inline comment for the one optional nit (duplicate `./worktree.js` import). No findings block merge.

title: refactor(#630): extract layered stage-command scaffold
